hypothesis_en: Ladies and gentlemen, the session is resumed. Because we're going to start the session. I ask you again to take your seats, because the session will begin again with the drawing of three scrutinizers to replace three Members who will not be able to exercise that mandate in the afternoon. Mr President, ladies and gentlemen, I would like to thank the rapporteur for his excellent report, and I would also like to thank the rapporteur for his excellent report. Mr Hannan, thank you very much, thank you very much, Mr Hannan, would you accept the post? I think you haven't heard me Mr. Hanna the letters I L Mr. or Mrs. Bersani Mr. Bersani, in that case, you have the letters M.O. and Mr. or Mrs. Muscat. Mr President, it is up to you to accept the ballot box with the letter 'S'. The scrutinizers who have helped us this morning, apart from the three whom we have now designated by lot, are still in office and they are entitled to the ballots in which they were this morning. Mr President, Mr Hanna, the letters I, L, Mr Bersani, the letters G, M and Mrs Muscat, the letter S. In accordance with the agenda, we are going to proceed to the election of the Vice-Presidents of the European Parliament. The following candidates are Mr Luigi Cosilovo, Mr Antonio Costa, Mr Ingo Friedrich, Mrs Silvia Yvonne Kaufmann, Mr Edward Macmillan Scott, Mr Mario Mauro, Mr Pierre Moscovici, Mr Gerard Onesta and the Mr President, ladies and gentlemen, I would like to thank the rapporteur, Mr Janusz Onyszkiewicz, Mr Miroslav Owski, Mrs Dagmar Rogberent, Mr Jacques Emil Sarjuskowski, Mr Antonio Trakatelis and Mr Alejo Vidal Cuadras, who have all expressed their support for this report. They were opposed to his candidacy and, since the number of candidates does not exceed the number of posts to be filled, I propose that the election be held by acclamation in accordance with Article 1 of the Rules of Procedure. Mr President, I therefore proclaim the candidates I have just appointed Vice-Presidents-elect of the European Parliament, and I congratulate them on this election. The Commission's proposal for a directive on the protection of workers from the risks related to exposure to carcinogens at work is based on the principle of proportionality. If you want to change your vote, ask for a new ballot paper to the voters.
